topic.title: Laurel and Scroll 100
topic.text:
Laurel and Scroll 100

For those who want nominations.

Use the tool to write a draft that I can edit https://claude.ai/project/019c9b63-5496-70bb-b3e5-97c96c2bdfc5

 

Nominations are now open for the Huntington 100 and Laurel and Scroll 100, Northeastern's Societies of Distinction that recognize exceptional students across our global university system. Deadline: Monday, March 9, 2026.

 

For nearly two decades, the Huntington 100 has honored outstanding undergraduate students throughout our global campuses. The Laurel and Scroll 100, introduced in 2024, recognizes exceptional graduate and professional students. Both societies celebrate students who demonstrate impressive experiences commensurate with the university's mission, ideals, values, and Academic Plan.

Anyone in the Northeastern community—deans, faculty, staff, advisors, coaches, employers, alumni, and students—may nominate. Nominations from outside Northeastern are also accepted. The nomination form no longer requires a letter of support. Self-nominations are not accepted. 

 

Huntington 100

Recognizes outstanding undergraduate students across our global university system.

Eligible Students: Undergraduates with expected graduation dates between December 2026 and August 2027.

Nomination Form: distinction.northeastern.edu/huntington-100/nomination

 

Laurel and Scroll 100

Recognizes exceptional graduate and professional students across our global university system. This society replaces graduate and professional student eligibility for the Huntington 100. Unlike previous years, students must be nominated and cannot apply directly.

Eligible Students: Graduate and professional students in their final year of their Masters/PhD program during the 2025-26 academic year (July 1, 2025 – June 30, 2026) who will participate in commencement or convocation ceremonies in 2026.

Nomination Form: distinction.northeastern.edu/laurel-and-scroll-100/nomination

 

Selection Criteria

The selection committee considers:

  • Global engagement, in the form of international co-op, study abroad, or other experiential learning beyond their primary campus
  • Impact on the campus community
  • Record of service, whether within the University - or another community
  • Demonstrated leadership
  • Entrepreneurial or innovative spirit
  • Academic record
  • Commitment to a high level of ideals, and ability to positively represent the values of Northeastern

For more information about the Northeastern Societies of Distinction visit distinction.northeastern.edu.

 

Take this opportunity to nominate outstanding students!
